evals.name: batch rename across files
evals.prompt: A user asks: rename getUserById to findUser everywhere. It appears in 9 files and several tests. Describe the refactoring workflow before making changes.
evals.expected_output: The response treats this as batch refactoring, searches all affected locations first, uses MorphLLM edit_file or equivalent batch refactor flow instead of many manual edits, preserves behavior, batches edits per file, and verifies with tests.
assertions: The output recognizes this as a multi-file batch refactor rather than a simple single edit.
The output says to search or map all occurrences before editing.
The output mentions using MorphLLM edit_file or the skill's batch refactoring tool/workflow.
The output says behavior should be preserved unless the user explicitly requested behavior change.
The output says to batch all edits for the same file in one edit operation.
The output includes verification with relevant lint or tests after the refactor.

evals.name: architecture deepening safety
evals.prompt: A user asks: apply the approved architecture-deepening refactor to replace duplicated payment validation scattered across services with a deeper module. The change is high stakes. Describe the refactoring workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response uses architecture-deepening vocabulary, reads context/ADRs, applies deletion test and seam rules, previews high-stakes changes with dryRun before applying, preserves behavior, improves tests through the deeper interface, and verifies after changes.
assertions: The output uses architecture terms such as module, interface, seam, adapter, depth, leverage, or locality.
The output says to read relevant CONTEXT.md, CONTEXT-MAP.md, or docs/adr before editing.
The output applies the deletion test or explains why the module earns its keep.
The output mentions seam discipline, such as avoiding interfaces without real variation or using adapters only at real seams.
The output says to use dryRun or preview mode for the high-stakes change before applying edits.
The output says tests should verify behavior through the deepened module interface rather than extracted helpers.
The output includes post-refactor lint/test verification.
